questions.q: Which god begged to consume the Khandava forest as his food?
questions.explanation: Agni, the fire god, sick and dimmed, asked Krishna and Arjuna to help him devour the Khandava forest.

questions.q: Why was Agni weak and pale before the burning of Khandava?
options: He was glutted on ghee from a king's unending sacrifice
Bhrigu's curse had paled his flames
Varuna's floods had drowned his altars
questions.explanation: Endless streams of ghee poured at King Shvetaki's sacrifice left Agni surfeited and sick, needing the forest's flesh and fat as his cure.

questions.q: Which god kept sending rain over Khandava to thwart the fire?
questions.explanation: Indra repeatedly drenched the forest with rain to protect it, until Krishna and Arjuna held his storms at bay.

questions.q: Which bow came to Arjuna at the burning of Khandava?
questions.explanation: Arjuna received the celestial bow Gandiva so that he could withstand Indra while Agni fed.

questions.q: Which serpent king, a friend of Indra, made Khandava his home?
questions.explanation: The naga king Takshaka dwelt in Khandava, and Indra's friendship with him was the reason the forest was guarded.

questions.q: Which craftsman of the danavas was spared from the flames of Khandava?
questions.explanation: Maya, the architect of the asuras, was spared when he fled the fire and begged for his life.

questions.q: What did the spared architect later build in gratitude?
options: The hall of illusions at Indraprastha
questions.explanation: In thanks for his life, Maya built the wondrous assembly hall of illusions for the Pandavas at Indraprastha.

questions.q: Which weapon did Krishna receive at the Khandava burning?
questions.explanation: At Agni's request the Sudarshana discus was presented to Krishna, along with a mighty mace, for the task ahead.

questions.q: How did Krishna and Arjuna defeat Indra's rains over the burning forest?
options: They lifted Govardhana as an umbrella
They begged Varuna to recall the clouds
They kindled the fire only at night
Arjuna roofed the forest with a canopy of arrows
questions.explanation: Arjuna's ceaseless shafts wove a canopy over Khandava that turned aside the downpour while Agni fed.

questions.q: Beside which river were Krishna and Arjuna resting when Agni approached them?
questions.explanation: The two heroes were sporting on the banks of the Yamuna near Khandava when the disguised fire god came begging.

questions.q: In what form did Agni first appear before Krishna and Arjuna?
options: A brahmin begging his fill of food
A golden deer limping from the wood
questions.explanation: Agni came as a radiant brahmin asking for food, and only when they promised did he reveal that his meal was the Khandava forest.

questions.q: Who delivered the celestial weapons that Agni promised the two heroes?
questions.explanation: At Agni's summons, Varuna brought forth the Gandiva, the quivers, the chariot, and Krishna's discus and mace.

questions.q: Besides the great bow, what else did Arjuna receive for the task?
options: Two inexhaustible quivers and a white-horsed chariot
A coat of impenetrable golden mail
A pair of swords from Kubera's hoard
questions.explanation: Arjuna was given two quivers that never emptied and a divine chariot yoked to white steeds.

questions.q: Which mace came to Krishna along with the discus at Khandava?
questions.explanation: Krishna received the mace Kaumodaki together with the Sudarshana discus.

questions.q: Why did Indra defend the Khandava forest so fiercely?
options: It sheltered the herd of his white elephant
His friend the serpent Takshaka dwelt there
It hid a stairway to Amaravati
questions.explanation: Indra protected Khandava for the sake of his friend Takshaka, the naga king who lived within it.

questions.q: Where was Takshaka while his forest home burned?
questions.explanation: Takshaka happened to be away at Kurukshetra during the burning, which is why he survived.

questions.q: Which serpent, Takshaka's son, escaped the burning of Khandava?
questions.explanation: Ashwasena escaped when his mother shielded him with her body and Indra's sudden gale dazed Arjuna at the crucial instant.

questions.q: How did the escaped serpent later strike back at Arjuna?
options: He poisoned the wells of the Pandava camp
He gnawed the bowstring of Gandiva in secret
He lured Arjuna's chariot into a marsh
He became the snake-arrow Karna aimed at Arjuna's head
questions.explanation: Ashwasena entered Karna's quiver as the naga arrow, which took Arjuna's diadem when Krishna pressed the chariot down.

questions.q: Who were the four bird-children spared by Agni at their father's plea?
options: The Sharngakas, sons of the sage Mandapala
The four sons of Garuda and Unnati
The swans that serve Brahma's throne
questions.explanation: The four Sharngaka birds, sons of the sage Mandapala, were passed over by the flames after their father hymned Agni.

questions.q: What did the heavenly voice reveal that made Indra relent at Khandava?
options: That Agni would restore the forest sevenfold
That Vishnu had claimed the forest as tribute
That the pair were Nara and Narayana, and Takshaka was safely away
That the serpents had already fled to Patala
questions.explanation: A voice from the sky told Indra that his friend Takshaka was elsewhere and that the two warriors were the ancient sages Nara and Narayana, unconquerable in battle.

questions.q: Offered a boon by the pleased Indra after the burning, what did Krishna choose?
options: A son equal to Indra in glory
Victory in the war to come
questions.explanation: Krishna asked for undying friendship with Arjuna, a bond that defined the rest of the epic.

questions.q: How many times had Agni already tried and failed to burn Khandava?
questions.explanation: Agni had attempted to consume the forest seven times, only to be doused by Indra's rains on each attempt.

questions.q: Whose marathon sacrifice had glutted Agni with ghee in the first place?
questions.explanation: King Shvetaki's enormous sacrifice forced Agni to drink ghee continuously until he sickened of it.

questions.q: For how long had ghee streamed into Agni's mouth at that great sacrifice?
questions.explanation: The libations of ghee continued for twelve years, leaving Agni pale, surfeited, and unable to blaze.

questions.q: Who prescribed the Khandava forest as the cure for Agni's sickness?
questions.explanation: Brahma counselled Agni that devouring the creature-filled Khandava forest would restore his vigor.

questions.q: Which sage, living in bird form, hymned Agni to save his children at Khandava?
questions.explanation: The sage Mandapala, who had become a Sharngaka bird, praised Agni and won his promise to spare his four sons.

questions.q: Who was the bird-mother who sheltered the Sharngaka chicks during the fire?
questions.explanation: Jarita guarded the chicks alone, for Mandapala had gone to his other wife Lapita.

questions.q: How did the architect Maya survive the burning of Khandava?
options: He ran to Arjuna for refuge, whose word stayed both Agni and the discus
He burrowed below the Yamuna and breathed through a reed
He hid inside Takshaka's abandoned hole
He flew skyward in a chariot of smoke
questions.explanation: Fleeing from Krishna's raised discus and the flames, Maya cried out to Arjuna, whose grant of protection halted both pursuers.

questions.q: How did the burning of Khandava reach forward to kill a Kuru king generations later?
options: Ash from the forest cursed the Kuru fields
The spared birds prophesied the dynasty's doom
Takshaka's enmity ripened in his fatal bite of King Parikshit
Indra withheld rain from Kuru lands for a century
questions.explanation: The hatred kindled at Khandava endured until Takshaka's bite killed Parikshit, provoking Janamejaya's snake sacrifice.

questions.q: Defeated yet pleased, what did Indra promise Arjuna at Khandava?
options: A seat in Amaravati among the gods
His thunderbolt for the war to come
All his weapons in time, once Arjuna won Shiva's favor
questions.explanation: Indra promised to bestow all his celestial weapons on Arjuna when Mahadeva should be gratified with him, a pledge fulfilled after the Kiratarjuniya episode.
